title: philotheca buxifolia subsp. obovata
intro: Philotheca buxifolia subsp. obovata, also known as the Tasmanian box myrtle, is a shrub native to Australia, specifically in the states of Tasmania and Victoria. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The health benefits of Philotheca buxifolia subsp. obovata include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a potential remedy for conditions like eczema, acne, and minor cuts and scrapes. Herbal preparations of Philotheca buxifolia subsp. obovata can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the leaves in a topical cream or salve to apply directly to affected areas.
